title: Albert de Rochas

# Albert de Rochas

## Overview

Albert de Rochas is the author of [[Unidentified Forces by Albert de Rochas]], lives on the outskirts of Paris in the [[Villa de Rochas]], and is connected to the [[The Ibroix]] salon/cult. He was once partly trained by [[The Master]] but broke with him over the philosophy of service.

## Appears In

- [[Newland Van Der Lynden]] - 2021-08-09
- [[Newland Van Der Lynden]] - 2021-10-11
- [[Newland Van Der Lynden]] - 2021-10-25

## Relationships

- [[The Master]] - former friend and partial teacher; they split over service and alien powers.
- [[Rachel Weissman]] - associated through [[The Ibroix]]; her address is found via Rochas's book.

## Associated Locations

- [[Villa de Rochas]]

## Associated Documents

- [[Unidentified Forces by Albert de Rochas]]
- [[Vistas Upon an Ocean of Endless Dark]]

## Notes

- The source says Rochas goes out on the town once a month, on the 15th.
- He is tied to a salon that sees itself as more than a cult.

title: Ariel Fontaine
text:
# Ariel Fontaine

## Overview

Ariel Fontaine is an investigator and journalist connected to a paper for which [[Herman]] later begins making cartoons. Her downtime focuses on the [[Duc de Roudier]], photographic evidence, [[Maribel Fontaine]], police suppression, and artistic circles.

## Downtime Timeline

### 2021-06-28

#### Summary
Ariel investigated the Duke, learning that he was widely loathed for arrogance and public indiscretions. The Duke's manor is in the [[Faubourg Saint-Germain Manor]], and he also keeps a flat at [[21 Avenue Montaigne]]. His wife, [[Orane Du Roudier]], lives at the manor and may have vital information. Ariel found a painting of the Duke, but no photograph.

#### People
- [[Duc de Roudier]]
- [[Orane Du Roudier]]

#### Places
- [[Faubourg Saint-Germain Manor]]
- [[21 Avenue Montaigne]]

#### Documents
- None named.

#### Clues and Discoveries
- The Duke was loathed for arrogance and indiscretions.
- The Duke has both a manor and a flat.
- [[Orane Du Roudier]] may know vital information.
- The archive notes a painting of the Duke, but no picture.

#### Open Threads
- What information does [[Orane Du Roudier]] have?
- Why is there no photograph of the Duke in the material Ariel found?

#### Source
- `009 - Downtimes for Ariel 6-28-21.md`

### 2021-06-28 (inferred)

#### Summary
Ariel investigated a strange photograph, [[Maribel Fontaine]], occult accounts of a red masked ghost or herald, a covered-up string of murders, and [[Maurice Maeterlinck]]. This is dated by inference from its proximity to Ariel's dated Duke investigation and the shared Fontaine/Duke investigation phase; the source file itself does not state a date.

#### People
- [[Maribel Fontaine]]
- [[Louis Lepine]]
- [[Maurice Maeterlinck]]

#### Places
- [[Paris]]

#### Documents
- [[Fontaine Investigation Photograph]]

#### Clues and Discoveries
- Different developing methods reveal different figures in the photograph: the commonly seen ghost, a Native American with a bullet wound, and a Serbian man.
- Maribel's father lives in Paris and works as a bureaucrat dealing with public works of the sewers.
- A newspaper notice says Maribel's father is looking for her because she has been missing for more than a week.
- Arcane texts describe a red-clad masked figure as possibly a blessing, avatar, herald, or manifestation of another creature.
- The gendarmerie have covered up murders of young women whose organs were taken; the cover-up appears motivated by fear of panic or incompetence, not confirmed complicity.
- Information about the murders goes through [[Louis Lepine]].
- [[Maurice Maeterlinck]] appears to be an established playwright with many art-scene contacts.

#### Open Threads
- Which figure in the photograph is most reliable, if any?
- Is the red masked figure empowered costume, herald, avatar, or creature?
- What does [[Louis Lepine]] know about the organ murders?

#### Source
- `020 - Ariels Investigation Downtimes.md`

title: Burk Vinet
text:
# Burk Vinet

## Overview

Burk Vinet is a head butler loyal to his master. The source distinguishes the real Burk from a shapeshifted Red Knight mask impersonation.

## Appears In

- [[Newland Van Der Lynden]] - 2021-08-09 (inferred)
- [[Newland Van Der Lynden]] - 2021-10-25

## Relationships

- [[Chloe]] - likely performs rituals with her.
- [[The Master]] - loyalty appears tied to the master of the house.
- [[Albert de Rochas]] - Burk knows about Rochas's earlier training under [[The Master]].

## Associated Locations

- [[Etoile Polaire Apartments]]

## Associated Documents

- [[Golden Water Lily]]
- [[Golden Mirror]]

## Notes

- Burk hides information out of honor and loyalty rather than clear malice.
- He is definitely a member of a cult, but the source does not identify which one.
- He and Chloe refuse absinthe.

title: Carcosa
text:
# Carcosa

## Overview

Carcosa is an alien or supernatural land repeatedly encountered through ritual, absinthe visions, mirror travel, crystals, and historical overlap with [[Corsica]].

## Appears In

- [[Molly]] - 2021-10-18
- [[Newland Van Der Lynden]] - 2021-10-25
- [[Newland Van Der Lynden]] - 2021-12-13

## Connected NPCs

- [[Marcel Proust]]
- [[Michel Gourand]]
- [[Jean-Leon Gerome]]
- [[Albert de Rochas]]

## Important Events

- Masters can enter and exit supernatural lands such as Carcosa through rituals.
- Newland experiences endless days in Carcosa as a Walker after looking into the [[Golden Mirror]] with absinthe.
- A ritual centuries ago caused an exchange between [[Corsica]] and Carcosa.
- The crystal shards come from Carcosan mines moved into this world.

## Notes

- The source preserves uncertainty around whether experiences in Carcosa are visions, travel, transformation, or some combination.

title: Carcosan Absinthe
text:
# Carcosan Absinthe

## Overview

An absinthe that enhances perception of Carcosan magic and gives dangerous insights, visions, or experiences.

## Appears In

- [[Newland Van Der Lynden]] - 2021-10-11
- [[Newland Van Der Lynden]] - 2021-10-25
- [[Newland Van Der Lynden]] - 2021-12-13

## Connected NPCs

- [[Burk Vinet]]
- [[Chloe]]

## Important Events

- Newland used it to view the Master's chamber and saw coffin-to-mirror travel.
- Newland used it with the [[Golden Mirror]] and experienced life as a Walker in [[Carcosa]].
- Newland used it with crystal shards and gained the Corsica/Carcosa insight.

## Notes

- Burk and Chloe refuse it, saying it opens understanding of power but is not a way to use power.
- Some uses require a push or yellow card in the source.

title: Crystal Shards from the Ibroix
text:
# Crystal Shards from the Ibroix

## Overview

Crystal shards associated with the [[The Ibroix]], [[Albert de Rochas]], and Carcosan mines. Light through them produces a deep yellow spectrum with occult effects.

## Appears In

- [[Newland Van Der Lynden]] - 2021-12-13
- [[Unidentified Forces by Albert de Rochas]]
- [[Herman]] - 2021-10-25, through the crystal lantern vision

## Connected NPCs

- [[Albert de Rochas]]
- [[Rachel Weissman]]

## Important Events

- Light through the shards can reveal or hold a dying creature's spirit-like figure.
- A mouse held in the light could not die by other means but still felt pain and healed normally.
- Absinthe insight says the crystals came from Carcosan mines moved into this world.

## Notes

- The source does not define the full limits of this effect.

# Dorian Van Der Lynden

![portrait](/wiki/media/Dorian-Van-Der-lynden.jpg)


*A gifted sculptor and former medical student whose greatest works seem to take on a life of their own.*

---

## Character Overview

**Name:** Dorian Van Der Lynden

A talented sculptor with formal medical training, Dorian abandoned one disciplined profession in pursuit of another. His artistic ambitions drive him to constantly push beyond his limits, though lately his greatest works have begun revealing something far stranger than mere inspiration. :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}

---

## Investigative Kit

**Field: Sculptor**

### Investigative Abilities

- Art History
- Military History
- Sculpture
- Steel
- Assess Honesty
- Demimonde
- Intuition
- Poetry

### General Kit

**Former Medical Student**

Your medical education may be behind you, but the knowledge and practical skills remain.

---

## General Abilities

| Ability | Rating |
|---------|------:|
| Athletics | 3 |
| Composure | 8 |
| Fighting | 5 |
| First Aid | 7 |
| Health (HP) | 3 |
| Mechanics | 0 |
| Riding | 2 |
| Sense Trouble | 2 |
| Sneaking | 3 |
| Preparedness | 1 |

---

## Drive

### Can-Do Attitude

> No challenge is impossible if you're willing to work hard enough. Obstacles exist to be overcome, not accepted.

---

## Relationships

### I Rely On

**Molly**

She seems capable of accomplishing anything she sets her mind to. Seeing her succeed gives you confidence that you can do the same.

---

### I Seek to Protect

**Regina**

You worry about the questionable people surrounding her and want to keep her away from the shady scoundrels who might exploit her.

---

## That Deuced Peculiar Business

Whenever you completely lose yourself in your work, something impossible happens.

The sculptures change.

Not dramatically, but subtly, as though someone has altered them while you slept.

More unsettling still are the leaves that gather around your studio.

Each morning they seem to arrange themselves into fragments of verse. Alone they are little more than disconnected sentences, but when assembled they become haunting poems.

The poetry is beautiful.

It is also far beyond anything you could consciously write.

Dark, melancholy, and strangely prophetic, the verses have become your most closely guarded secret.

---

## Notes

- Pushes: **2**

title: Dulcinea Fountaine
summary: A well traveled French nouveau riche who is still seen as "middle class" by her vastly wealthier friend
text:
# Dulcinea "Dee" Fountaine

![portrait](/wiki/media/Dulcinea-Fountaine.jpg)

*A well traveled French nouveau riche who is still seen as "middle class" by her vastly wealthier friend*




---

## Character Overview

**Name:** Dulcinea "Dee" Fountaine  
**Nationality:** French  
**Background:** Former poor shopkeeper's daughter whose family inherited wealth from a deceased uncle. Despite her newfound fortune, she remains an outsider among Europe's true elite.

---

## Investigative Kit

**Ballerina / Dancer**

### Investigative Abilities

- Bonhomie
- Fashion
- Miscellany
- Reassurance
- Society
- Assess Honesty
- **Local (France)** - A special ability representing your French upbringing and your ability to relate to fellow countrymen.

### General Kit

**Raised on the Streets**

Your family lived the humble life of poor shopkeepers until an uncle passed away, leaving your family unexpectedly wealthy. While your circumstances changed overnight, your instincts never did.

---

## General Abilities

| Ability | Rating |
|---------|------:|
| Athletics | 5 |
| Composure | 6 |
| Fighting | 7 |
| First Aid | 1 |
| Health (HP) | 4 |
| Mechanics | 2 |
| Riding | 0 |
| Sense Trouble | 3 |
| Sneaking | 3 |
| Preparedness | 4 |

### Fighting Style

Your combat style reflects your dance training, relying on graceful kicks, spins, agility, and precise movement rather than brute strength.

---

## Drive

### Yen for Discovery

> "Civilization depends on the accumulation and mastery of knowledge. As a trained artist, I understand this sort of thing better than those stuff-heads in the academy. I'll prove it by making discoveries they are too timid to uncover for themselves!"

---

## Relationships

### I Rely On

**Newland (Michael)**

He seems like the least frightening person when situations become dangerous. His quiet confidence and silence are oddly comforting.

**Herman (Daniel)**

He reminds you of your father, especially with the endless supply of food stuffed into his pockets. His presence feels reassuringly familiar.

---

### I Seek to Protect

**Regina (Jenn)**

You have seen far too many young "muses" manipulated and discarded. You want to help Regina make wise decisions, knowing firsthand that wealth cannot solve every problem.

**Dorian (Josh)**

A close friend of yours died because of alcohol. You worry Dorian may be heading down a similar path and hope he can find healthier ways to cope before it is too late.

---

## That Deuced Peculiar Business

For months you suffered vivid dreams of sacrifice, exhaustion, and your very will being drained away.

Last week, a terrifying realization struck.

They were never dreams.

A coven of witches had been secretly stealing your life essence, drugging your drinking water so you would sleep through their rituals. Suspecting something was wrong, you secretly replaced your water without alerting them.

When they gathered once more, you remained awake.

As the witches became absorbed in their ritual, you fled into the rain.

Cold, terrified, and unsure who would believe your impossible story, you considered the one place that might offer refuge.

By the time you climbed the steps of the **North Star**, the rain had begun to let up.

# Fontaine Investigation Photograph

## Overview

A photograph that changes depending on development method and appears in Ariel's and Molly's investigations.

## Full Text or Extract

Ariel found that different developing methods reveal different images. One version shows the commonly perceived ghost. A second shows a Native American with a bullet hole in his chest. A third shows a Serbian man. Ariel has copies of each alternate photo.

title: From the Sun Comes Shadows
text:
# From the Sun Comes Shadows

## Overview

An investigation note involving [[Le Soleil d'Or]], [[Lucien Valadin]], [[Pauline]], and a commission delivered by a Serbian-looking messenger. The date is inferred from its Le Soleil d'Or context and nearby early investigation material; the source handout itself has no explicit date.

## Full Text or Extract

The investigator went to [[Le Soleil d'Or]] to find [[Lucien Valadin]]. [[Pauline]], the owner or keeper of the place, was evasive until told that Lucien was being sought for a commission. Pauline then gave Lucien's address and said Lucien had not come to the bar for weeks, though he normally conducted business there.

The last time Lucien came in, he met a Serbian-looking man who said he was the messenger for Lucien's latest commission. The man handed over a satchel with sketches and photos. Lucien told Pauline the setup was very odd and that, if the price had not been excessive, he likely would not have entertained such distance from his client. That was the last time Lucien was seen at Le Soleil d'Or.

## Found By / Connected To

- [[Le Soleil d'Or]] - 2021-08-09 (inferred)
- [[Lucien Valadin]]

## Related NPCs

- [[Pauline]]
- [[Lucien Valadin]]

## Related Clues

- [[Serbian Messenger Commission]]

title: Herman
text:
# Herman

## Overview

Herman is an artist who begins producing cartoons for Ariel's paper. His downtime establishes a mundane income source, a firearm purchase, and an emerging method for intentionally producing prophetic illustrations.

## Downtime Timeline

### 2021-10-25

#### Summary
Herman started drawing cartoons for the paper connected to [[Ariel Fontaine]], earning a minor income and improving his French through cartoon balloons. He bought a Chamelot-Delvigne Model 1873 revolver with ammunition. He also tried to intentionally connect with the force or person on the other side that causes his prophetic illustrations.

#### People
- [[Ariel Fontaine]]

#### Places
- None named.

#### Documents
- [[Prophetic Cartoons]]

#### Clues and Discoveries
- Herman's cartoons sometimes show the future when drawn in his sleep.
- He caught himself drawing a lantern with crystal-focused light shining on a strapped-down man.
- The prophetic cartoons are made at 11:30 at night.

title: Herman Wentworth
text:
# Herman "Art" Wentworth

![portrait](/wiki/media/Herman-Wentworth.jpg))

*A newspaper cartoonist whose strange prophetic drawings have led him across the Atlantic in search of answers.*

---

## Character Overview

**Name:** Herman "Art" Wentworth *(Arminius is the Latin form of Herman)*

After graduating from Princeton, Herman joined the **New York Times**, expecting a career writing thoughtful articles and observations. Instead, he was assigned to the newspaper's cartoon desk. Under the tutelage of a veteran cartoonist, Herman discovered an unexpected artistic talent. While the Wentworth family considered cartooning a childish profession, eccentrics are hardly uncommon among the Wentworths. :contentReference[oaicite:1]{index=1}

---

## Investigative Kit

**Newspaper Cartoonist**

### Investigative Abilities

- Painting
- Research
- Assess Honesty
- Bonhomie *(Making Friends)*
- Belle-Lettres *(Journalism)*
- Inspiration

### General Kit

**Scrounger**

Your knack for always having exactly the item you and your companions need occasionally requires a flexible interpretation of traditional property rights.

---

## General Abilities

| Ability | Rating |
|---------|------:|
| Athletics | 3 |
| Composure | 7 |
| Fighting | 6 |
| First Aid | 0 |
| Health (HP) | 3 |
| Mechanics | 0 |
| Riding | 1 |
| Sense Trouble | 3 |
| Sneaking | 5 |
| Preparedness | 5 |

---

## Drive

### Curiosity

> "When I smell a mystery, I must solve it. No one must get between me and the truth."

---

## Relationships

### I Rely On

**Newland (Michael)**

As a military man, Newland seems like the sort of person who can get you out of trouble when things go wrong.

---

### I Seek to Protect

**Regina (Jenn)**

Your cousin and childhood friend. You came to Paris because you fear for her safety and intend to make sure nothing happens to her.

**Ariel Fontaine (Carla)**

As a fellow newspaper professional, Ariel reminds you of others who have been exploited in the industry. You want to ensure she doesn't become another victim.

---

## That Deuced Peculiar Business

One evening you fell asleep at your drawing table while working late.

When you awoke, an entire series of cartoons lay before you.

They depicted your cousin Regina being brutally attacked and murdered with a hatchet.

At first you dismissed it as exhaustion.

Then it happened again.

Every few nights you awaken to new drawings, each depicting Regina and strangers you have never met caught in horrifying and impossible events.

You have never consciously drawn any of them.

Without revealing the true reason, you convinced your family to let you travel to Paris under the pretense of pursuing a "real" career.

In truth, you came to discover why your hands continue to create these dreadful visions while you sleep.

---

## Notes

- Obtained a firearm, legally registered under your own name.

title: Meredith Katherine Cooper
text:
# Meredith Katherine "Molly" Cooper

![portrait](/wiki/media/Meredith-Katherine-Molly-Cooper.jpg)

*An optimistic tomboy and the only daughter of a wealthy Oklahoma ranching family, sent to Paris to become a proper lady but far more interested in adventure than etiquette.*

---

## Character Overview

**Name:** Meredith Katherine "Molly" Cooper

The only child of enormously successful Oklahoma ranchers, Molly was raised with hard work, open skies, and little patience for high society. Her parents, determined to turn their spirited daughter into a respectable young lady, sent her to Paris to study watercolor painting, hoping she would acquire refinement, social grace, and perhaps a wealthy husband.

Molly, however, sees the world as an adventure waiting to be explored.

---

## Investigative Kit

**Landscape Painter**

*Student of the Landscape of Life • Watercolors*

### Investigative Abilities

- Architecture
- Art History
- Natural History
- Negotiation
- Painting
- Occult *(Spiritualism)*

### Special Ability

- May take a **Yellow Card** to remain awake and fully functional for **one continuous week**.

### General Kit

**Raised on a Farm**

Whether owner or laborer, you grew up in the healthy air and demanding work of rural life. Years of physical labor have made you stronger and hardier than most city folk.

---

## General Abilities

| Ability | Rating |
|---------|------:|
| Athletics | 6 |
| Composure | 8 |
| Fighting | 6 |
| First Aid | 3 |
| Health (HP) | 5 |
| Mechanics | 0 |
| Riding | 4 |
| Sense Trouble | 0 |
| Sneaking | 0 |
| Preparedness | 1 |

---

## Drive

### Family Curse

> "This weird situation reminds me of some strange hints my grandfather once dropped, before his unexplained disappearance. This is finally my opportunity to comprehend our so-called ancestral doom!"

---

## Relationships

### I Rely On

**Regina (Jenn)**

Regina's fiercely independent nature inspires Molly to question the social expectations she has always accepted. It's one thing to rebel against the system. It's another thing entirely to ignore it.

**Herman (Daniel)**

When Herman gazes into the mystical mirror, he becomes Henry. Whatever the explanation, Molly cannot ignore what she sees.

---

### I Seek to Protect

**Ariel (Carla)**

Molly sees Ariel as the sister she never had. She wants to teach her that destiny is something people forge for themselves, not something written in the stars.

**Newland (Michael)**

The war has left deep scars on Newland. Whether from those memories or from the strange connection to Henry's old room, Molly believes he possesses a gentle soul that deserves guidance rather than judgment.

---

## That Deuced Peculiar Business

Molly traveled to Paris accompanied by her older brother, **Henry**, who was to serve as her chaperone.

He arranged everything.

Their accommodations.

Their luggage.

Their rooms.

Together they settled into the **Étoile Polaire Apartments** at **20 Rue Théodule Ribot**, known to its residents as **The North Star**.

Then, two weeks later...

Henry was gone.

Not merely missing.

His luggage had vanished.

His room no longer existed.

No one remembered him.

The landlord insisted Molly had always arrived alone. The neighbors stared blankly when she spoke his name. Even letters from home made no mention of a second child.

Most horrifying of all, their parents no longer remembered ever having had a son.

Molly cannot explain what has happened.

But it feels disturbingly familiar, echoing whispered stories of disappearances and forgotten relatives that have haunted the Cooper family for generations.

She is determined to find Henry...

...and unravel whatever ancient curse has begun claiming her family.

The war has left deep scars on Newland. Whether from those memories or from the strange connection to Henry's old room, Molly believes he possesses a gentle soul that deserves guidance rather than judgment.

---

## That Deuced Peculiar Business

Molly traveled to Paris accompanied by her older brother, **Henry**, who was to serve as her chaperone.

He arranged everything.

Their accommodations.

Their luggage.

Their rooms.

Together they settled into the **Étoile Polaire Apartments** at **20 Rue Théodule Ribot**, known to its residents as **The North Star**.

Then, two weeks later...

Henry was gone.

Not merely missing.

His luggage had vanished.

His room no longer existed.

No one remembered him.

The landlord insisted Molly had always arrived alone. The neighbors stared blankly when she spoke his name. Even letters from home made no mention of a second child.

Most horrifying of all, their parents no longer remembered ever having had a son.

Molly cannot explain what has happened.

But it feels disturbingly familiar, echoing whispered stories of disappearances and forgotten relatives that have haunted the Cooper family for generations.

She is determined to find Henry...

...and unravel whatever ancient curse has begun claiming her family.

# Molly

## Overview

Molly's downtime focuses on Masters, [[Marcel Proust]], [[Michel Gourand]], and the truth or uncertainty around [[Jean-Leon Gerome]], [[The Red Knight]], and the [[The Warton Players]].

## Downtime Timeline

### 2021-10-18

#### Summary
Molly visited [[Marcel Proust]] for Russian-style tea to ask where [[Michel Gourand]] might have gone, why, and what it means to be a Master of a cult.

#### People
- [[Marcel Proust]]
- [[Michel Gourand]]

#### Places
- [[Carcosa]]

#### Documents
- None named.

#### Clues and Discoveries
- Proust says all Masters can enter and exit supernatural lands through rituals.
- The source specifically names [[Carcosa]] as one of those lands.
- It is likely Gourand had goals he had to pursue in other worlds.

#### Open Threads
- Where did [[Michel Gourand]] go?
- What goals required travel to other worlds?

#### Source
- `005 - Downtimes for Molly 10-18-21.md`

### 2021-10-25

#### Summary
Molly investigated [[Jean-Leon Gerome]], concluding he did not personally comport with the [[The Warton Players]]. Evidence suggests he was elsewhere during events attributed to him, while a double or doppelganger appeared as [[The Red Knight]].

#### People
- [[Jean-Leon Gerome]]
- [[Adele Fontaine]]
- [[Mr. Fontaine]]
- [[Dorian Van Der Lynden]]

#### Places
- Performance sites connected to [[The King in Yellow (Performances)]]

#### Documents
- [[Fontaine Investigation Photograph]]
- [[The King in Yellow (Performances)]]

#### Clues and Discoveries
- Gerome has at least seven documented pieces of evidence that he was not at locations he was suspected of attending.
- Gerome seemed aware of a double or doppelganger appearing only as [[The Red Knight]].
- His married paramour, [[Adele Fontaine]], took a photograph of one such event and was later found dead by Gerome.
- It could be suspected that [[Mr. Fontaine]] was in attendance and may have killed Adele, but this is marked as suspicion only.
- Gerome has recurring dreams of Carcosa without knowing the name.
- He has dreamed roughly thirty times of Native Americans connected to Newland's material rending him or the Red Knight to pieces; over time the attackers have dwindled to five men, and the Knight continues to stand.
- The dream changed after the revival of Candida where the first rows of the audience died.
- Molly records a theory that Gerome was strong enough not to participate, so someone or something made a stand-in; Dorian is not as strong and is being consumed by it.

#### Open Threads
- Who or what made the Red Knight stand-in?
- What changed in Gerome's dream after Candida?
- How literal is Molly's conclusion that Dorian is being consumed?

#### Source
- `004 - Downtimes for Molly 10-25-21.md`

title: Newland Ainsley Van der Lyden
text:
# Newland Ainsley Van der Lyden

![portrait](/wiki/media/Newland-Ainsley-Van-der-Lyden.png)

*A former American Reserve Officer turned poet whose fascination with forbidden knowledge has begun to blur the line between inspiration and obsession.*

---

## Character Overview

**Name:** Newland Ainsley Van Der Lyden

Born into a wealthy California family of wine producers, Newland joined the American Reserve Officer Corps to satisfy his parents' expectations. After completing his service, he found himself increasingly drawn toward forgotten lore, occult mysteries, and forbidden knowledge. Rather than pursuing another respectable career, he began expressing these unsettling fascinations through poetry, seeking truth in places most people refuse to look. :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}

---

## Investigative Kit

**Poet**

### Investigative Abilities

- Demimonde
- Intuition
- Occult
- Poetry
- Bonhomie
- Assess Honesty

### General Kit

**Former Cadet (Reserve Officer)**

Military training has left you disciplined, physically capable, and accustomed to remaining calm under pressure.

---

## General Abilities

| Ability | Rating |
|---------|------:|
| Athletics | 6 |
| Composure | 6 |
| Fighting | 7 |
| First Aid | 2 |
| Health (HP) | 3 |
| Mechanics | 0 |
| Riding | 3 |
| Sense Trouble | 2 |
| Sneaking | 2 |
| Preparedness | 2 |

---

## Drive

### Seeker of Forbidden Truth

> "I find vibrant color and truth in the esoteric and macabre literature that inspires me."

---

## Relationships

### I Rely On

**Ariel Fontaine (Carla)**

Her fascination with the occult mirrors your own. When confronting the unknown, you trust her instincts more than anyone else's.

---

### I Seek to Protect

**Dorian Van Der Lynden**

Whether by blood or family legacy, you feel responsible for Dorian's future and will not allow harm to come to him if it can be prevented.

---

## That Deuced Peculiar Business

Ever since your military service, your nights have belonged to another man.

He is someone you killed in the line of duty.

He never accuses you.

He never threatens you.

Instead, he simply whispers.

Each dream brings strange phrases in languages you have never studied, fragments of occult knowledge that somehow linger in your memory after waking. The words feel ancient, dangerous, and impossibly meaningful.

You cannot decide whether they are the product of guilt...

...or whether the dead have truly found a way to speak.

---

## Occult Knowledge

### Spell

**Basic Warding with Salt**

- Cost: **1 Health**
- Cost: **1 Composure**

A simple protective ritual that creates a ward against unnatural forces.

### Other Knowledge

- Able to translate **Carcosan Runes** with a successful Composure roll.
- Keeps a personal supply of absinthe close at hand.


title: Newland Van Der Lynden
text:
# Newland Van Der Lynden

## Overview

Newland Van Der Lynden is an occult investigator whose downtimes track the mechanics of [[Carcosan Absinthe]], [[Kaladi Bedur]], [[The Ibroix]], [[Albert de Rochas]], [[Marcel Proust]], Carcosan artifacts, and the dangerous overlap between Paris, [[Corsica]], and [[Carcosa]].

## Downtime Timeline

### 2021-08-09

#### Summary
Newland investigated [[Kaladi Bedur]], [[Louise Weber]], trunk runes, the [[Villa de Rochas]], and the creature in the Gold Room.

#### People
- [[Louise Weber]]
- [[Albert de Rochas]]

#### Places
- [[Villa de Rochas]]

#### Documents
- [[Junk in the Trunk Runes]]

#### Clues and Discoveries
- [[Louise Weber]] is not a leader of [[Kaladi Bedur]]; she was likely a manipulated victim who joined after being opened to its secrets.
- [[Kaladi Bedur]] began as an Egyptian cult contacted by an otherworldly entity.
- Its sacrifices do not always die; some are aged by eleven and a half years and then brought into the cult.
- Members of the cult seem immune to drunkenness or poison.
- Louise has had many younger chambermaids disappear; she claims they moved up to better positions.
- Kaladi Bedur has an underworld contract to remove children under eleven and a half years old for 1000 francs.
- The trunk runes are pictorial and some phrases resemble language heard in the opera.
- The Gold Room's gold seems to keep energy in; Newland wonders whether monsters can be killed with gold.
- The Gold Room creature was not created or controlled by Rochas; it fed on energies in the room and crossed into the world when the group came near.

#### Open Threads
- What is the otherworldly entity communicating with [[Kaladi Bedur]]?
- Can gold harm or contain the Gold Room creature?
- What do the trunk runes fully say?

#### Source
- `008 - Downtimes for Newland 8-9-21.md`

### 2021-08-09 (inferred)

#### Summary
Newland investigated [[Le Soleil d'Or]] and the real [[Burk Vinet]], distinguishing him from a Red Knight shapeshifted mask. This date is inferred as an early Newland downtime near the other August investigation material; the source file itself has no explicit date.

#### People
- [[Burk Vinet]]
- [[Chloe]]

#### Places
- [[Le Soleil d'Or]]

#### Documents
- None named.

#### Clues and Discoveries
- [[Le Soleil d'Or]] hosted occultists and societies; about a year earlier a conflict between cults produced curses and losses among members.
- Loan sharks prey on unlucky patrons there.
- The poetry at Le Soleil d'Or seems unusually strong and mood-reflective, perhaps because of magic inspiring performers.
- The Burk seen in the first half of the prior game was not Burk but a shapeshanged mask of the man in red.
- The real Burk is hiding something out of honor and loyalty to his master rather than clear malice.
- Burk is definitely in a cult, likely doing rituals with [[Chloe]], but those rituals do not seem harmful from the source.

#### Open Threads
- What cult is Burk part of?
- What remains physically searchable at [[Le Soleil d'Or]]?

#### Source
- `011 - Downtimes for Newland.md`

### 2021-10-11

#### Summary
Newland used [[Carcosan Absinthe]] at [[Etoile Polaire Apartments]] to investigate [[Michel Gourand]], questioned [[Dulcinea Fontaine]]'s condition, and searched for [[Vistas Upon an Ocean of Endless Dark]].

#### People
- [[Michel Gourand]]
- [[Dulcinea Fontaine]]
- [[Rachel Weissman]]
- [[Albert de Rochas]]

#### Places
- [[Etoile Polaire Apartments]]
- [[Ecole des Beaux-Arts]]

#### Documents
- [[Vistas Upon an Ocean of Endless Dark]]

#### Clues and Discoveries
- A vision showed the Master getting out of a coffin and walking through a mirror reflection.
- Dulcinea was staying at school while preparations continued around the clock; she seemed exhausted but not truly out of sorts and did not know what had happened.
- Only two copies of [[Vistas Upon an Ocean of Endless Dark]] are known: one from the writer and one from the apprentice.
- Newland believes one copy is held by [[Rachel Weissman]] and the other likely by [[Albert de Rochas]].

#### Open Threads
- What happened to [[Michel Gourand]] after entering the mirror reflection?
- What is in [[Vistas Upon an Ocean of Endless Dark]]?

#### Source
- `007 - Newland - Downtimes for 10-11-21.md`

### 2021-10-25

#### Summary
Newland investigated [[Marcel Proust]], the relationship between [[Albert de Rochas]] and [[The Master]], the [[Golden Water Lily]], and the [[Golden Mirror]].

#### People
- [[Marcel Proust]]
- [[Albert de Rochas]]
- [[The Master]]
- [[Burk Vinet]]
- [[Chloe]]

#### Places
- [[Carcosa]]

#### Documents
- [[Unidentified Forces by Albert de Rochas]]

#### Clues and Discoveries
- Proust has occult protections tied to his house and doors; he is not among the most powerful Masters but can hold back attacks in his home.
- Proust is gradually going mad and has three yellow cards as he enters a mania to stop the Ibroix from killing people in Paris while fearing for his own life.
- The Master tried to recruit and partly train Albert in his youth, but they split over the philosophy of service.
- Burk and Chloe say the [[Golden Water Lily]] can be empowered by destroying Carcosan artifacts into it or by letting it rest in the grave of a cultist or creature for one night of a full moon until eleven cultist energies are taken.
- Burk and Chloe refuse absinthe, seeing it as a way to understand power rather than use it.
- Newland drank absinthe before the [[Golden Mirror]], fell asleep, and experienced life in [[Carcosa]] as a Walker. Six hours passed in the real world, but he remembered what felt like endless days.

#### Open Threads
- Can Proust stop the Ibroix without collapsing into mania?
- What is the safest or least destructive way to empower the [[Golden Water Lily]]?
- What does becoming or perceiving oneself as a Walker imply?

#### Source
- `002 - Downtimes for Newland 10-25-21.md`

### 2021-12-13

#### Summary
Newland experimented with [[Crystal Shards from the Ibroix]], investigated [[Kaladi Bedur]] leadership, researched [[Doctor Sasha Kersiki]], followed up on [[Phillip Du Pol]] and the [[The Warton Players]], and questioned [[Jean-Leon Gerome]]'s connection to [[Dulcinea Fontaine]].

#### People
- [[Doctor Sasha Kersiki]]
- [[Louis Lepine]]
- [[Phillip Du Pol]]
- [[Jean-Leon Gerome]]
- [[Dulcinea Fontaine]]
- [[Rachel Weissman]]

#### Places
- [[Grand Hotel]]
- [[Ecole des Beaux-Arts]]
- [[Corsica]]
- [[Carcosa]]

#### Documents
- [[Unidentified Forces by Albert de Rochas]]
- [[The King in Yellow (Performances)]]
- [[Sunrise Order King in Yellow Version]]

#### Clues and Discoveries
- Normal light through the crystals shines in a deep yellow spectrum, and the prismed light appears to have occult effects.
- A mouse killed under the light produced a small, meek spirit held in the light; while held it could not die by other means but felt pain and healed normally.
- Absinthe insight suggested that centuries ago Corsica suffered a ritual similar to the Paris-Carcosa ritual: part of Corsica went to Carcosa, and part of Carcosa came to Corsica.
- The crystals are from mines of Carcosa that moved into this world.
- [[Kaladi Bedur]] is publicly criminal, known for assassinations, kidnappings, and blackmail.
- [[Doctor Sasha Kersiki]] resides at the [[Grand Hotel]], uses multiple names and disguises, and has been seen at [[Ecole des Beaux-Arts]] at odd hours.
- A Greek Orthodox monk described the [[Sunrise Order]], a heretical sect whose visions produced a King in Yellow-like text.
- Gerome thinks Dulcinea may have become pregnant; he appears not to be part of occult happenings at the school.
- Newland found no connection between Gerome and Dulcinea's predicament beyond limited prior contact.

#### Open Threads
- What practical limits govern the crystal light's power over death and pain?
- Is the Corsica/Carcosa name similarity a surviving echo of the old ritual?
- What is [[Doctor Sasha Kersiki]] doing at the art school?
- What is the relationship between the Sunrise Order text and later King in Yellow performances?

#### Source
- `000 - Downtimes for Newland 12-13-21.md`

- [[Rachel Weissman]]

#### Places
- [[Grand Hotel]]
- [[Ecole des Beaux-Arts]]
- [[Corsica]]
- [[Carcosa]]

#### Documents
- [[Unidentified Forces by Albert de Rochas]]
- [[The King in Yellow (Performances)]]
- [[Sunrise Order King in Yellow Version]]

#### Clues and Discoveries
- Normal light through the crystals shines in a deep yellow spectrum, and the prismed light appears to have occult effects.
- A mouse killed under the light produced a small, meek spirit held in the light; while held it could not die by other means but felt pain and healed normally.
- Absinthe insight suggested that centuries ago Corsica suffered a ritual similar to the Paris-Carcosa ritual: part of Corsica went to Carcosa, and part of Carcosa came to Corsica.
- The crystals are from mines of Carcosa that moved into this world.
- [[Kaladi Bedur]] is publicly criminal, known for assassinations, kidnappings, and blackmail.
- [[Doctor Sasha Kersiki]] resides at the [[Grand Hotel]], uses multiple names and disguises, and has been seen at [[Ecole des Beaux-Arts]] at odd hours.
- A Greek Orthodox monk described the [[Sunrise Order]], a heretical sect whose visions produced a King in Yellow-like text.
- Gerome thinks Dulcinea may have become pregnant; he appears not to be part of occult happenings at the school.
- Newland found no connection between Gerome and Dulcinea's predicament beyond limited prior contact.

#### Open Threads
- What practical limits govern the crystal light's power over death and pain?
- Is the Corsica/Carcosa name similarity a surviving echo of the old ritual?
- What is [[Doctor Sasha Kersiki]] doing at the art school?
- What is the relationship between the Sunrise Order text and later King in Yellow performances?

#### Source
- `000 - Downtimes for Newland 12-13-21.md`

title: Regina Wentworth
text:
# Regina Wentworth

## Overview

Regina Wentworth's downtime uses fashion and social research to identify supernatural evidence around old shoes and to connect the [[Duc de Roudier]] to Cassilda, [[Ecole des Beaux-Arts]], and [[The King in Yellow (Performances)]].

## Downtime Timeline

### 2021-06-28 (inferred)

#### Summary
Regina examined shoes and researched the [[Duc de Roudier]]. The shoes were handmade, classic or expertly made, and made from materials at least a century old. She learned that the Duke financed Cassilda almost completely and that his family supported students at [[Ecole des Beaux-Arts]] for the last performance of [[The King in Yellow (Performances)]]. This date is inferred from the adjacent Duke investigation material; the source file itself has no explicit date.

#### People
- [[Duc de Roudier]]

#### Places
- [[Chateau de Roudier]]
- [[Ecole des Beaux-Arts]]

#### Documents
- [[The King in Yellow (Performances)]]

#### Clues and Discoveries
- The shoes are solid proof that the cause was not a member of the house and was likely supernatural.
- The Duke is informally known as the Duke and lives at [[Chateau de Roudier]] outside the city.
- The Duke financed Cassilda almost completely.
- The family gave a grant to Ecole students to make the last performance of The King in Yellow.
- Many people who claim to have seen him did not see his face; they saw him leaving parties or watching performances from curtained private boxes.

#### Open Threads
- Who or what wore the old shoes?
- Why is the Duke's face so consistently unseen?

#### Source
- `010 - Downtimes for Regina Wentworth.md`

# Sunrise Order King in Yellow Version

## Overview

A King in Yellow-like text attributed to visions recorded by clergy connected to the heretical [[Sunrise Order]].

## Full Text or Extract

A Greek Orthodox monk told Newland that church legends describe a heretical sect called the Sunrise Order. They believed Jesus was imprisoned by God because mankind was not ready for salvation. The Sunrise Order performed rites to connect to Jesus in the "prison of God." Through visions, one of the clergy wrote down a version of what one would consider The King in Yellow. The sect believed deep understanding of it could reveal a way to free him and let him bring the whole world into his splendor.

title: The 7 Lines of the Fountaine Family
text:
# The 7 Lines of the Fountaine Family



## Details
- Paris – Little information is known, the last promoted member of the family Julius, was burned at the stake on August 8 1793.
- Marseille – The Marseille Branch were bankers and merchants; they grew very rich from wise investments. The leader of the branch is Bertrand Fontaine.
- Lyon – The Lyon branch owned textile factories that are the archetype of industrial workhouse.  Adoline Fontaine is the widowed head of the family.
- Toulouse – The Toulouse family is known for lavish parties where they make their money is a question as everyone seems to have a different answer. Art or Underworld practices seem to be the most common tales; Marco Fontaine is the head of this branch.
- Nice – A bastard line, it is believed a house maid of Julius became pregnant and taking some of the wealth left behind in Paris started a home in Nice for her child. They right now make their money in canned fish.
- Strasbourg – The second bastard line, seems to come from Julius brother Charles who’s lover left the Paris to her family's home. They ran an armory that supplied weapons both to the Germans and the French.
- American - This is Ariel's line.

## Related

- [[Ariel Fontaine]]
- [[Dulcinea Fountaine]]
- [[Maribel Fontaine]]

:::gm
The truth behind the myth.
:::

title: The King in Yellow (Performances)
text:
# The King in Yellow (Performances)

## Overview

A performance history of The King in Yellow centered on the [[The Warton Players]], the disastrous 1870 performances, and a third rehearsal at the [[Grand Hotel]].

## Full Text or Extract

The Warton Players performed The King in Yellow twice in 1870. The first performance was praised as a triumph of writing. During the second performance, a fire broke out in the hall and 300 people died. The government declared the writing itself against God and the body politic, and within about three weeks it is believed all copies were destroyed.

The source notes that the first performance coincided with Emperor Napoleon III's capture, while the fire coincided with republicans taking over the country. It also questions how the new government could have gathered all copies so quickly while also trying to root out royalists.

A contact reports a third performance: a dress rehearsal before the opening, not in the main venue but in the ballroom of [[Grand Hotel]], 12 Boulevard des Capucines. Some performers may not have been the same as the main-show performers.

## Found By / Connected To

- [[Molly]] - 2021-10-25
- [[Regina Wentworth]] - 2021-06-28 (inferred)
- [[Newland Van Der Lynden]] - 2021-12-13

## Related NPCs

- [[Phillip Du Pol]]
- [[Jean-Leon Gerome]]
- [[Duc de Roudier]]

## Related Clues

- [[The Red Knight]]
- [[Sunrise Order King in Yellow Version]]

A contact reports a third performance: a dress rehearsal before the opening, not in the main venue but in the ballroom of [[Grand Hotel]], 12 Boulevard des Capucines. Some performers may not have been the same as the main-show performers.

title: The Medical Case of Maribel Fontaine
text:
# The Medical Case of Maribel Fontaine

## Overview

A medical handout describing [[Maribel Fontaine]]'s death at [[Hotel-Dieu]], unauthorized surgery by [[Doctor Sasha Kersiki]], organ removal, and an egg found inside her body. The date is inferred from the surrounding Fontaine investigation material; the source handout itself has no explicit date.

## Full Text or Extract

The investigator returned to Hotel-Dieu after eight days and found Maribel apparently asleep in bed, then realized she was dead. Examination showed surgery wounds not present from the original attack. Someone appeared to have removed organs from her.

Hospital staff, cowed by the investigator's authority, said [[Doctor Sasha Kersiki]] came and operated on her. Because the investigator had operated on Maribel rather than a staff doctor, the staff did not feel authorized to stop him. The body was moved to the morgue for autopsy.

The autopsy raised more questions than it answered. Maribel's heart had been removed in a way that damaged her lungs, and it made no sense that she was not cold when found. In the place of the kidney previously removed by the investigator, there was a fist-sized egg. When raised to the light, something could be seen inside it.

## Found By / Connected To

- [[Maribel Fontaine]]
- [[Hotel-Dieu]]
- 2021-06-28 (inferred)

## Related NPCs

- [[Maribel Fontaine]]
- [[Doctor Sasha Kersiki]]

- [[Organ Murders]]
- [[Maribel Fontaine Egg]]

title: Unidentified Forces by Albert de Rochas
text:
# Unidentified Forces by Albert de Rochas

## Overview

A text by [[Albert de Rochas]] about technology, crystals, photography, waves of other-dimensional energy, and supernatural perception.

## Full Text or Extract

Plain reading: The book deals with using technology to sense the supernatural. Crystals have power because waves of other-dimensional energy pass through them. Native cultures and tribal groups once saw these effects, but most knowledge has been lost until the rise of modern technology. Things invisible to human eyes may be visible through other photographic methods.

Intuition reading: The ideas seem to answer questions as they arise, bringing the darkness into the light in a way that feels different from faith-based occult study.

Occult reading: A story describes researchers studying death who learned that light passing through a particular gemstone and shining on a dying person allowed them to see that person's grim reaper, called charos. As long as the light touched it, the person would not die.

Demimonde reading: Rochas has a cult, though they see themselves more as a salon, called [[The Ibroix]]. They meet once every spring and fall. One address is tracked down: [[Rachel Weissman]]. Rochas lives on the outskirts of Paris at [[Villa de Rochas]] and goes out on the 15th of each month.

## Found By / Connected To

- [[Newland Van Der Lynden]]

## Related NPCs

- [[Albert de Rochas]]
- [[Rachel Weissman]]

## Related Clues

- [[Crystal Shards from the Ibroix]]
- [[Charos and Death Light]]
